Learn Django

Home · Real-World Use Cases
Home Use Cases Games Projects

15 Production Use Cases

Real-world patterns used in production Django applications at companies like Instagram, Disqus, Mozilla, The Washington Post, and Eventbrite. Each card includes code patterns and key implementation details.

01 / API

REST API with Django REST Framework

Instagram, Mozilla, Eventbrite
class ArticleViewSet(viewsets.ModelViewSet):
    queryset = Article.objects.all()
    serializer_class = ArticleSerializer
    permission_classes = [IsAuthenticatedOrReadOnly]
    filter_backends = [DjangoFilterBackend, OrderingFilter]
    filterset_fields = ['category', 'status']
    ordering_fields = ['pub_date', 'title']

# urls.py
router = DefaultRouter()
router.register('articles', ArticleViewSet)
urlpatterns = [path('api/', include(router.urls))]
  • ModelViewSet provides full CRUD with 5 lines of config
  • Serializers handle validation, nested relationships, and field selection
  • Built-in pagination, throttling, versioning, and content negotiation
  • Browsable API for development and debugging
DRF ViewSets Serializers
02 / CMS

Blog / Content Management System

Wagtail, WordPress alternatives
class Article(models.Model):
    title = models.CharField(max_length=200)
    slug = models.SlugField(unique=True)
    body = models.TextField()
    author = models.ForeignKey(User, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    category = models.ForeignKey(Category, on_delete=models.SET_NULL, null=True)
    tags = models.ManyToManyField(Tag, blank=True)
    status = models.CharField(max_length=10,
        choices=[('draft','Draft'),('published','Published')])
    pub_date = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    featured_image = models.ImageField(upload_to='articles/', blank=True)

    class Meta:
        ordering = ['-pub_date']
  • Rich model relationships: ForeignKey, ManyToMany, auto-generated slugs
  • Admin customization with list_display, search_fields, list_filter
  • Template inheritance for consistent layouts across pages
  • RSS feed generation with django.contrib.syndication
Models Admin Templates
03 / COMMERCE

E-Commerce Platform

Saleor, Oscar, Shopify alternatives
class Order(models.Model):
    user = models.ForeignKey(User, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    items = models.ManyToManyField(Product, through='OrderItem')
    status = models.CharField(max_length=20, default='pending')
    total = models.DecimalField(max_digits=10, decimal_places=2)
    created = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)

    @transaction.atomic
    def complete_order(self):
        for item in self.orderitem_set.all():
            item.product.stock -= item.quantity
            item.product.save()
        self.status = 'completed'
        self.save()
        send_order_confirmation.delay(self.id)  # Celery task
  • Transaction-safe order processing with @transaction.atomic
  • Intermediate model (through table) for order line items
  • Stripe/PayPal integration via Django signals
  • Inventory management with select_for_update() to prevent overselling
Transactions Celery Signals
04 / DATA

Data Dashboard

Newsrooms, Analytics teams
class DashboardView(LoginRequiredMixin, TemplateView):
    template_name = 'dashboard.html'

    def get_context_data(self, **kwargs):
        ctx = super().get_context_data(**kwargs)
        ctx['articles_by_month'] = (
            Article.objects
            .annotate(month=TruncMonth('pub_date'))
            .values('month')
            .annotate(count=Count('id'))
            .order_by('month')
        )
        ctx['top_authors'] = (
            Author.objects
            .annotate(article_count=Count('articles'))
            .order_by('-article_count')[:10]
        )
        return ctx
  • ORM aggregation with annotate(), TruncMonth, Count, Sum, Avg
  • Class-based views with mixins for auth and context
  • JSON API endpoints for feeding D3/Chart.js visualizations
  • Cached querysets for expensive dashboard computations
Aggregation CBVs Caching
05 / AUTH

Authentication System

Every production Django app
class CustomUser(AbstractUser):
    email = models.EmailField(unique=True)
    avatar = models.ImageField(upload_to='avatars/', blank=True)
    role = models.CharField(max_length=20,
        choices=[('editor','Editor'),('admin','Admin'),('viewer','Viewer')])

    USERNAME_FIELD = 'email'
    REQUIRED_FIELDS = ['username']

# settings.py
AUTH_USER_MODEL = 'accounts.CustomUser'
AUTHENTICATION_BACKENDS = [
    'django.contrib.auth.backends.ModelBackend',
    'allauth.account.auth_backends.AuthenticationBackend',
]
  • Custom user model with email-based authentication
  • Django-allauth for OAuth (Google, GitHub, Twitter)
  • Role-based permissions with custom permission classes
  • JWT tokens for API authentication via djangorestframework-simplejwt
Custom User OAuth JWT
06 / ASYNC

Celery Task Queue

Instagram, Pinterest
# tasks.py
@shared_task(bind=True, max_retries=3)
def process_upload(self, file_id):
    try:
        upload = Upload.objects.get(id=file_id)
        upload.status = 'processing'
        upload.save()

        # Heavy processing
        thumbnail = generate_thumbnail(upload.file)
        upload.thumbnail = thumbnail
        upload.status = 'complete'
        upload.save()
    except Exception as exc:
        self.retry(exc=exc, countdown=60)

# views.py
def upload_view(request):
    upload = Upload.objects.create(file=request.FILES['file'])
    process_upload.delay(upload.id)  # Non-blocking
    return JsonResponse({'task_id': upload.id})
  • Offload heavy work (email, image processing, reports) to background workers
  • Automatic retry with exponential backoff on failure
  • Periodic tasks with celery-beat for scheduled jobs
  • Redis or RabbitMQ as the message broker
Celery Redis Background Jobs
07 / REALTIME

WebSocket Chat Application

Slack alternatives, live dashboards
# consumers.py
class ChatConsumer(AsyncWebsocketConsumer):
    async def connect(self):
        self.room_name = self.scope['url_route']['kwargs']['room']
        self.room_group = f'chat_{self.room_name}'
        await self.channel_layer.group_add(self.room_group, self.channel_name)
        await self.accept()

    async def receive(self, text_data):
        data = json.loads(text_data)
        await self.channel_layer.group_send(
            self.room_group,
            {'type': 'chat.message', 'message': data['message'],
             'username': self.scope['user'].username}
        )

    async def chat_message(self, event):
        await self.send(text_data=json.dumps(event))
  • Django Channels extends Django with WebSocket support
  • Channel layers (Redis-backed) for cross-process communication
  • Async consumers for non-blocking real-time messaging
  • Integration with Django auth for authenticated WebSocket connections
Channels WebSocket Redis
08 / SAAS

Multi-Tenant SaaS Platform

Enterprise apps, B2B products
class TenantMiddleware:
    def __init__(self, get_response):
        self.get_response = get_response

    def __call__(self, request):
        hostname = request.get_host().split('.')[0]
        try:
            request.tenant = Tenant.objects.get(subdomain=hostname)
        except Tenant.DoesNotExist:
            return HttpResponse('Tenant not found', status=404)
        return self.get_response(request)

class TenantAwareManager(models.Manager):
    def get_queryset(self):
        from threading import local
        _thread = local()
        return super().get_queryset().filter(
            tenant=getattr(_thread, 'tenant', None)
        )
  • Subdomain-based tenant isolation via custom middleware
  • Shared database with tenant FK (shared schema) or django-tenants for separate schemas
  • Custom model managers that auto-filter by tenant
  • Tenant-aware caching, file storage, and email configuration
Middleware Multi-tenant Managers
09 / ADMIN

Custom Admin Dashboard

Every Django project
@admin.register(Article)
class ArticleAdmin(admin.ModelAdmin):
    list_display = ['title', 'author', 'status', 'pub_date', 'word_count']
    list_filter = ['status', 'category', 'pub_date']
    search_fields = ['title', 'body']
    prepopulated_fields = {'slug': ('title',)}
    date_hierarchy = 'pub_date'
    readonly_fields = ['created_at', 'updated_at']
    inlines = [CommentInline, ImageInline]
    actions = ['publish_selected', 'export_as_csv']

    def word_count(self, obj):
        return len(obj.body.split())
    word_count.short_description = 'Words'

    def publish_selected(self, request, queryset):
        queryset.update(status='published')
    publish_selected.short_description = 'Publish selected articles'
  • Custom list views with computed columns and admin actions
  • Inline editing for related models (comments, images)
  • Date hierarchy for temporal browsing
  • Custom admin views for dashboards, reports, and batch operations
Admin Inlines Actions
10 / FILES

File Upload Pipeline

Media platforms, document management
class Document(models.Model):
    file = models.FileField(upload_to=upload_path)
    content_type = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    size = models.PositiveIntegerField()
    checksum = models.CharField(max_length=64)

def upload_path(instance, filename):
    ext = filename.split('.')[-1]
    return f'uploads/{date.today():%Y/%m}/{uuid4().hex}.{ext}'

# Custom storage backend for S3
class S3Storage(Storage):
    def _save(self, name, content):
        s3_client.upload_fileobj(content, BUCKET, name)
        return name

# settings.py
DEFAULT_FILE_STORAGE = 'myapp.storage.S3Storage'
FILE_UPLOAD_MAX_MEMORY_SIZE = 10 * 1024 * 1024  # 10MB
  • Dynamic upload paths with UUID filenames to prevent collisions
  • Custom storage backends for S3, GCS, or Azure Blob
  • File validation: size limits, content type checks, virus scanning
  • Chunked uploads for large files with django-chunked-upload
FileField S3 Storage
11 / SEARCH

Full-Text Search with PostgreSQL

Content-heavy sites, documentation
from django.contrib.postgres.search import (
    SearchVector, SearchQuery, SearchRank, TrigramSimilarity
)

def search_articles(query):
    vector = SearchVector('title', weight='A') + \
             SearchVector('body', weight='B')
    search_query = SearchQuery(query, search_type='websearch')

    return (
        Article.objects
        .annotate(rank=SearchRank(vector, search_query))
        .filter(rank__gte=0.1)
        .order_by('-rank')
    )

# Trigram similarity for fuzzy matching
def suggest_articles(query):
    return Article.objects.annotate(
        similarity=TrigramSimilarity('title', query)
    ).filter(similarity__gt=0.3).order_by('-similarity')
  • PostgreSQL full-text search built into Django (no Elasticsearch needed)
  • Weighted search vectors: title matches rank higher than body matches
  • Trigram similarity for fuzzy/typo-tolerant search
  • GIN indexes for fast search performance on large datasets
SearchVector PostgreSQL Trigram
12 / GRAPHQL

GraphQL API with Strawberry

Frontend-driven apps, mobile APIs
import strawberry
from strawberry.django import type as django_type

@django_type(models.Article)
class ArticleType:
    id: strawberry.auto
    title: strawberry.auto
    body: strawberry.auto
    author: 'AuthorType'
    pub_date: strawberry.auto

@strawberry.type
class Query:
    @strawberry.field
    def articles(self, category: str | None = None) -> list[ArticleType]:
        qs = Article.objects.select_related('author')
        if category:
            qs = qs.filter(category__slug=category)
        return qs

schema = strawberry.Schema(query=Query)
  • Type-safe schema with Python type hints (Strawberry) or class-based (Graphene)
  • Clients request exactly the fields they need, reducing over-fetching
  • Automatic N+1 prevention with DataLoader pattern
  • Subscriptions for real-time updates via WebSocket
Strawberry GraphQL Type Safety
13 / CACHE

Caching Strategies

High-traffic sites
# 1. View-level caching
@cache_page(60 * 15)  # 15 minutes
def article_list(request):
    return render(request, 'list.html', {'articles': Article.objects.all()})

# 2. Template fragment caching
# {% cache 300 sidebar request.user.id %}
#   ... expensive template block ...
# {% endcache %}

# 3. Low-level cache API
def get_article(pk):
    key = f'article:{pk}'
    article = cache.get(key)
    if article is None:
        article = Article.objects.get(pk=pk)
        cache.set(key, article, timeout=3600)
    return article

# 4. QuerySet caching with invalidation
from django.db.models.signals import post_save
@receiver(post_save, sender=Article)
def invalidate_cache(sender, instance, **kwargs):
    cache.delete(f'article:{instance.pk}')
    cache.delete('article_list')
  • Four caching layers: per-view, template fragment, low-level API, and database
  • Cache invalidation via Django signals on model save/delete
  • Redis backend for shared cache across multiple workers
  • Cache versioning and key prefixing for multi-site deployments
cache_page Redis Signals
14 / TEST

Testing Patterns

CI/CD pipelines, production confidence
class ArticleAPITests(APITestCase):
    def setUp(self):
        self.user = User.objects.create_user('reporter', password='testpass')
        self.client.force_authenticate(self.user)
        self.article = Article.objects.create(
            title='Test Article', author=self.user, status='published'
        )

    def test_list_articles(self):
        response = self.client.get('/api/articles/')
        self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 200)
        self.assertEqual(len(response.data['results']), 1)

    def test_create_article_requires_auth(self):
        self.client.logout()
        response = self.client.post('/api/articles/', {'title': 'New'})
        self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 401)

    @patch('myapp.tasks.process_article.delay')
    def test_publish_triggers_task(self, mock_task):
        response = self.client.patch(
            f'/api/articles/{self.article.id}/', {'status': 'published'}
        )
        mock_task.assert_called_once_with(self.article.id)
  • TestCase with automatic database rollback per test
  • Factory Boy for generating test fixtures
  • Mocking external services (Celery tasks, API calls) with unittest.mock
  • Coverage reports and CI integration with GitHub Actions
TestCase Factory Boy Mocking
15 / DEPLOY

Deployment Architectures

Production infrastructure
# Dockerfile
FROM python:3.12-slim
WORKDIR /app
COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt
COPY . .
RUN python manage.py collectstatic --noinput

# docker-compose.yml
services:
  web:
    build: .
    command: gunicorn myproject.wsgi:application --bind 0.0.0.0:8000 --workers 4
    env_file: .env
    depends_on: [db, redis]
  db:
    image: postgres:16
    volumes: [pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data]
  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
  celery:
    build: .
    command: celery -A myproject worker -l info
  nginx:
    image: nginx:alpine
    ports: ['80:80', '443:443']
  • Docker Compose for local development matching production
  • Gunicorn with multiple workers behind Nginx reverse proxy
  • Environment variables for secrets (never in settings.py)
  • Health checks, zero-downtime deploys, and database migrations in CI
Docker Gunicorn Nginx CI/CD
